nodes.label: AI as research and tech spec assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or forgetful assistant

nodes.label: Human-led requirements gathering
nodes.grounding_span: getting the requirements fleshed out and written down. That's the hardest part

nodes.label: AI memory and consistency
nodes.label_negative: AI forgetting details and repeating mistakes
nodes.grounding_span: AI keeps 'forgetting' one of the detail in my document or my previous message

nodes.label: Conversation length and context window
nodes.grounding_span: if the conversation is too lengthy, there's a high chance that it will go above the context limit

nodes.label: AI as helpful assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI as primary or autonomous agent
nodes.grounding_span: It will be a helpful assistant

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: time is invaluable for me and for the business

nodes.label: Quality and reliability of AI output
Human judgment and contextual understanding
nodes.grounding_span: it requires understanding business context, for both AI haven't come close to become an effective assistant yet

nodes.label: Frustration with AI forgetting details
nodes.grounding_span: AI keeps 'forgetting' one of the detail in my document or my previous message

nodes.label: Pragmatic acceptance of AI limitations
nodes.grounding_span: If it's too bad I'd start a new conversation and summarize what we have achieved

nodes.label: Openness to future AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I may consider using it more extensively

nodes.label: Structured upfront preparation — detailed context provision before AI interaction
nodes.grounding_span: I try to make the PRD as detailed and with as much background context as possible

nodes.label: Iterative feedback loop — structured rounds of research, feedback, and analysis
nodes.grounding_span: I told them to do research first, then come back to me with some promising tools/architectures/frameworks, then I provide feedback
